name: Escalona - Cenicientos - Pelahust
name_original: Pelahustán y Cenicientos – circular desde Escalona
description: A moderate 33.6-mile road cycling route from Escalona, passing through Pelahustán and Cenicientos with 2630 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Escalona offer diverse landscapes characterized by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and rural countryside. The region features significant elevation changes, providing challenging ascents and rewarding descents for cyclists. Riders can expect to pass through lush, green areas, including patches of farmland and forests, alongside historic villages. Best road cycling routes around Escalona The most popular road cycling route is Cenicientos, Cadalso de los Vidrios y Almorox – circular from Escalona, a 29.1 miles (46.8 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 15 minutes to complete, connecting several charming towns. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra de San Vicente – circular from Escalona via Castillo de Bayuela and Pelahustán, a difficult 54.9 miles (88.4 km) path. This route features substantial climbs and panoramic views of the Sierra de San Vicente. Local road cyclists also love the Hiking and cycling in Pelahustán and Cenicientos – circular route from Escalona, a 33.6 miles (54.1 km) trail leading through peaceful countryside and villages, often completed in about 2 hours 37 minutes. Road cycling around Escalona is defined by mountainous areas, river valleys, and historic villages. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The routes in Escalona are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Escalona's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Escalona?
A: There are over a dozen dedicated road cycling routes around Escalona, offering a variety of experiences. These routes cater to different fitness levels, ranging from moderate to difficult.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Escalona?
A: The region is characterized by diverse landscapes, including mountainous terrain with significant elevation changes, picturesque river valleys, and peaceful rural countryside. You'll cycle through lush, green areas, patches of farmland, and fragrant forests, often on well-paved surfaces.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes in Escalona?
A: Yes, Escalona offers several challenging routes with substantial climbs and rewarding descents. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029307254/sierra-de-san-vicente-circular-from-escalona-via-castillo-de-bayuela-and-pelahustan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de San Vicente – circular from Escalona via Castillo de Bayuela and Pelahustán&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 88.4 km path featuring nearly 1000 meters of elevation gain, offering panoramic views.

Q: Are there any easier road cycling routes for beginners in Escalona?
A: While many routes involve elevation, there are moderate options suitable for those looking for a less intense ride. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20135489" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Escalona – Detour to Aldea en Cabo loop from Escalona&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 41.3 km route that provides a good introduction to the area's cycling.

Q: What landmarks or points of interest can I see along road cycling routes in Escalona?
A: Many routes pass by historical and cultural landmarks. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430530" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Escalona Castle Palace&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cenicientos Historic Center&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable castles like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Silvestre Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430519" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de la Vela&lt;/a&gt; are also in the vicinity.

Q: Are there any historic villages to visit while road cycling near Escalona?
A: Absolutely. Road cycling routes often connect charming towns and villages where you can experience local culture. Rides from Escalona can lead you to places like Cenicientos, Cadalso de los Vidrios, Almorox, Castillo de Bayuela, and Pelahustán, perfect for a coffee stop.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Escalona?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Escalona are circular, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029231858/cenicientos-cadalso-de-los-vidrios-y-almorox-circular-from-escalona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cenicientos, Cadalso de los Vidrios y Almorox – circular from Escalona&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 46.8 km loop.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Escalona?
A: The road cycling routes in Escalona are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, challenging climbs, and the opportunity to explore historic villages and scenic landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Escalona?
A: The region generally offers good conditions for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide pleasant temperatures, making them ideal for longer rides. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended, while winters are generally mild but can have cooler temperatures.

Q: Are the road cycling routes around Escalona suitable for families?
A: While many routes involve significant elevation and distance, some moderate options might be suitable for families with older, experienced cycling members. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029263155/hiking-and-cycling-in-pelahustan-and-cenicientos-circular-route-from-escalona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking and cycling in Pelahustán and Cenicientos – circular route from Escalona&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 54.1 km route through peaceful countryside, which could be considered for fitter families.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Escalona?
A: Parking is generally available in Escalona town center, which serves as a common starting point for many routes. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival, especially during peak seasons.

Q: Is public transport available for cyclists in Escalona?
A: Public transport options in Escalona are primarily bus services. While some buses may accommodate bicycles, it's recommended to check with the specific bus operator beforehand regarding their policy on carrying bikes, especially for longer journeys or during busy times.
tips: Pelahustán is one of those places whose name has several possible origins, and each of the theories tells a part of the history of the town. The most accepted relates the name to the fustan fabric that was produced here, either with linen or with a mixture of silk and cotton. This cloth was coarse and rough and was used by hunters as protection, and the expression "pelar el fustán" existed as a synonym for hunting, but there was also a theory that the town was called Pelafustán with a derogatory character, like where the pelafustanes live ( rascals). On the other hand, a simpler theory is that there was a settler of these lands called Pela Hustán. Nothing to see.

Located at one of the extreme points of the Community of Madrid, this town is known for its vineyards and natural beauty. The Camino de Santiago del Sureste passes from Alicante, Cartagena, Murcia, Valencia, Albacete and Toledo. Throughout the entire town you will be able to see beautiful granite stone fountains for public use.

Initially conceived as a Roman fortress, it was later taken over by the Muslims to finally come under the orders of Alfonso VI of Castile and became a Gothic castle with a Mudejar-style interior palace.
It is worth visiting inside and appreciating this interesting contrast of architectural styles, admission is free.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is approximately 54.1 kilometers (33.6 miles) long. Cyclists can expect to complete it in around 2 hours and 37 minutes, requiring a sustained effort.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features mostly well-paved surfaces that are easy to ride. However, as a moderate difficulty route, be prepared for the possibility of some unpaved or more challenging segments.

faqs.question: Is this circular route suitable for beginner cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate and requires good fitness due to its length and an elevation gain of over 800 meters. It demands a sustained effort, so it might be challenging for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal details are not provided, generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant weather for cycling in this region, avoiding the extreme heat of summer or potential chill of winter.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or historical sites along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through areas with historical interest. You can visit the Main Square of Pelahustán, explore the Cenicientos Historic Center, and see the Escalona Castle Palace.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route starting from Escalona, you would typically look for parking options within Escalona itself to begin your ride. Consider parking near the town center or accessible areas close to the route's start.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling, which generally means dogs are not typically brought along on the bike itself. If you are considering bringing a dog, it would be more suitable for walking sections, but the primary activity is cycling.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public road cycling route through Pelahustán and Cenicientos starting from Escalona.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route coincide with other official paths. Notably, about 25% of the route follows the Camino de Santiago del Sureste, and smaller portions intersect with the Vereda de Ganados.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food or drinks along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through towns like Pelahustán, Cenicientos, and Escalona, you will find opportunities to stop for refreshments, cafes, or restaurants in these settlements.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while cycling?
faqs.answer: While specific details for this exact route are not extensively provided, the area around Pelahustán has historical interest. Cycling routes in Spain often feature diverse landscapes, including charming villages and scenic environments, suggesting a potentially picturesque ride.
